zh.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Margaret McLean於大約1700年出生在美國賓夕法尼亞州。她於1723年與Michael Clingan在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是6個兒子的父母。她去世於1813年3月在她的家鄉享年114歲。

nameMeanings.name: McLean
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Gille Eáin, ‘son of the servant (i.e. devotee) of (Saint) John’. Compare Irish McAloon and McGlone .

History: This is the name of a major Scottish clan traditionally associated with the island of Mull, which claims descent from Gille Eáin na Tuaidh (‘Gillean of the Battleaxe’), who lived in the 13th century.

variants.name: McClean
meanings: Irish (northern): variant of   McLean  .

variants.name: Lean
meanings: English (mainly Devon): nickname for a thin or lean person, from Middle English lene ‘lean’ (Old English hlǣne).
Shortened form of Scottish   McLean  .
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 林, see   Lin   1.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: Formed from Margaret.

historicEvents.description: Thomas Jefferson's American Declaration of Independence endorsed by Congress. Colonies declare independence.
The Declaration of Independence was signed in Philadelphia on July 4, 1776. The liberty bell was first rung here to Celebrate this important document.

historicEvents.title: The First Presidential Election
historicEvents.description: The First Presidential election was held in the newly created United States of America. Under the Articles of Confederation, the executive branch of the country was not set up for an individual to help lead the nation. So, under the United States Constitution they position was put in. Because of his prominent roles during the Revolutionary War, George Washington was voted in unanimously as the First President of the United States.

historicEvents.title: Whiskey Rebellion Occurs
historicEvents.description: The Whiskey Rebellion was a protest against taxes on whiskey by farmers and distillers. People were tarred and feathered. They attacked Bower Hill and headed towards Pittsburgh. The militia was sent in to try and stop it. It finally ended with a repeal of the tax.

en.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Margaret McLean was born about 1700, in Pennsylvania, United States. She married Michael Clingan in 1723, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 6 sons. She died in March 1813, in her hometown, at the age of 114.

fr.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Margaret McLean est née environ 1700, à Pennsylvanie, États-Unis. Elle a épousé Michael Clingan en 1723, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils. Elle est décédée en mars 1813, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 114 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Margaret McLean wurde um 1700 in Pennsylvania, Vereinigte Staaten geboren. Sie hat 1723 in England, United Kingdom Michael Clingan geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne. Sie starb im März 1813 im Alter von 114 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Margaret McLean è nata il circa 1700 nel Pennsylvania, Stati Uniti d'America. Lei sposato Michael Clingan nel 1723, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli. Morì nel marzo 1813, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 114 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Margaret McLean(은)는 대략 1700년 미국 펜실베이니아 주에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1723년 영국 잉글랜드에서 Michael Clingan(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1813년 3월 그녀의 고향에서 114세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Margaret McLean родилась около 1700 г. в местности Пенсильвания, Соединенные штаты. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Michael Clingan в 1723 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей. Она умерла марта 1813 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 114 лет.
